statusRule: Delivery stages control sequencing. Legacy calendar dates and week labels are reference slots only; they do not activate work or prove completion. Status changes require owner decisions and evidence.

stages.title: Admin UI Construction
stages.goal: Build the approved Admin flows and states against contract-shaped fixtures without waiting for Mobile or Backend implementation.
stages.exit: Admin UI cards pass component, permission, accessibility, fixture-state and recovery tests; real-system integration remains explicitly owned by Stage 3.

stages.title: Mobile and Backend Construction
stages.goal: Build authoritative Backend behavior and Mobile journeys while preserving the contracts already used by Admin fixtures.
stages.exit: Backend and Mobile cards through their construction scope have accepted contracts, tests and staging evidence ready for Admin integration.

stages.title: Admin Integration
stages.goal: Replace Admin fixtures with real permissioned Backend contracts and persisted Mobile outcomes, domain by domain.
stages.exit: All five Admin integration cards pass real-contract, stale-state, authorization, audit and end-to-end recovery checks.

stages.title: Cross-Platform Hardening and Release
stages.goal: Run cross-platform integration, security, performance, recovery, release-candidate and freeze work after Admin integration.
stages.exit: Release evidence, operational dry runs, critical-defect decisions and the controlled build freeze are accepted.

weeks.title: Foundation, Identity and Contract Availability
weeks.goal: Create the shared project foundations and unblock mobile and admin with stable auth, profile, PIN, session, admin auth and dashboard contracts.
weeks.exit: Mobile can complete the app-entry, authentication and profile shell; Admin can enter the protected dashboard shell; Backend has stable response and error shapes plus an OpenAPI shell.

weeks.title: Tasker Activation, Task Creation and Funding
weeks.goal: Build the flows that allow Taskers to become eligible and Task Owners to create, locate, attach media to and fund tasks.
weeks.exit: Tasker activation and KYC, catalog, task draft, location, media, payment intent and broadcast or direct-offer contracts are usable on staging.

weeks.title: Discovery, Matching and Active Task Start
weeks.goal: Build public discovery, Tasker interest, Task Owner selection and the first half of active execution.
weeks.exit: A funded task can move from broadcast or direct offer through Tasker interest, Task Owner acceptance and active-task start with chat and tracking foundations.

weeks.title: Completion, Exceptions, Trust and Finance
weeks.goal: Close the task lifecycle with completion, settlement, cancellation, no-show, reports, ratings, favorites, wallet, withdrawal and support.
weeks.exit: A task can finish cleanly or enter a governed exception path; affected wallet, payout, report and admin-review states are visible.

weeks.title: Operational Integration, Recovery and Full-Flow QA
weeks.goal: Complete secondary flows, operational admin coverage, provider validation, recovery behavior and end-to-end flow QA.
weeks.exit: All planned flows have working happy paths and documented exception paths on staging with defect lists by flow or module.

weeks.title: Hardening, Security and Performance
weeks.goal: Fix integration defects, harden performance, security and provider behavior, improve recovery paths and close major technical gaps.
weeks.exit: Critical Week 5 defects are resolved or owned, and core flows are stable enough for release-candidate preparation.

weeks.title: Release Candidate Preparation and Operational Dry Runs
weeks.goal: Prepare Mobile, Admin and Backend release candidates while running provider, payout, monitoring, backup and support dry runs.
weeks.exit: Release candidates are available on staging with provider-validation evidence, backup-restore proof and operational handover notes.

weeks.title: Stabilization, Go-Live Rehearsal and Build Freeze
weeks.goal: Complete the final defect pass, go-live rehearsal, documentation handover and controlled build freeze before formal testing starts.
weeks.exit: Builds are frozen for the two-week testing window. Only blocker fixes are allowed after this point.
